posts.text: I wasn't previously worried about alarmist claims that the American economy had basically gone all-in on an AI bet, but I am now.

2011 - AWS outage brings down half the internet. Engineers grumble, keep working.

2018 - GitHub outage breaks pipelines and slows collaboration. Engineers grumble, keep working offline.

2026 - Claude goes down. Engineers stare blankly at terminals, realize they forgot how to do their own jobs.

This isn't a "detention center". It's a concentration camp occupied by people who were kidnapped in violation of their due process and whose civil rights continue to be ignored in spite of court orders.

There WILL be accountability for every single horror committed in trump's name.

one big difference: in my version, I'm applying the detector before the rotation, and then leveraging GT mappings for pairwise representational similarity.

Running the detector on the rotated image makes sense: in my approach, that'd prob be a second pass with a reversed rotation.

reply_context.text:
x'=Rot(x)
z,z'=f(x),f(x')
c,c'=g(z,y),g(z',y)
L=c+c'

what I'm suggesting:
L=c+c' +dist(z,z')

I strongly suspect adding a term like this to your objective would accelerate training.

posts.text:
